modules.purpose: Publishes the Claude marketplace descriptor for the TORQUE plugin bundle and points the marketplace entry at the local plugin source.
Defines the Claude plugin manifest, including the TORQUE MCP server launch command and the packaged hooks, skills, agents, and commands.
Provides default runtime settings for the standalone TORQUE agent, including its bind address, command allowlist, project root, and concurrency limit.
Implements the standalone TORQUE agent HTTP service with secret-based auth, guarded command execution, git sync, capability probing, and peek proxying.
Declares the standalone TORQUE agent package as a small Node ESM entrypoint rooted at agent/index.js.
Exercises the standalone agent /run endpoint, covering NDJSON streaming, allowlist and path guards, timeout handling, concurrency limits, stderr forwarding, and TORQUE_-prefixed env passthrough.
Verifies the standalone agent HTTP server contract for auth, /health metrics, missing-route handling, and /projects discovery.
Validates the /sync workflow against disposable git repositories, including default branch selection, error paths, and project registry updates surfaced by /health and /projects.

Implements the top-level TORQUE CLI entrypoint, printing help and version output, dispatching core commands to the CLI modules, and translating budget, cache, template, plan, and backup subcommands into API tool calls.
Provides the shared CLI HTTP client, including timeout-aware fetch wrappers, response parsing, and normalized API and network errors for TORQUE server requests.
Normalizes CI-related CLI arguments and forwards status, watch, stop, diagnose, run listing, and provider configuration requests to the matching TORQUE tool endpoints.
Implements the main Node CLI command handlers, validates user input, dispatches REST and tool calls, delegates CI subcommands, and polls task completion for the await flow.
Builds the local dashboard URL from the configured port and opens it in the platform-default browser with execFile-based process spawning.
Runs CLI environment diagnostics for Node, the TORQUE server, dashboard, MCP SSE endpoint, Ollama, optional external CLIs, API keys, and `.mcp.json`, then prints a summarized report.
Converts raw server markdown and text responses into readable CLI output, including colored status summaries, parsed tables, task previews, submission views, and fallback rendering.
Scans for Ollama, local CLI tools, and configured API keys, then writes workspace `.mcp.json` and `.env` templates unless existing files are preserved without `--force`.
Centralizes the shared `~/.torque` paths, API endpoint defaults, and PID read or cleanup helpers used by local lifecycle commands.
Launches `server/index.js` in foreground or detached mode, ensures `~/.torque` exists, rewrites stale PID state, records `torque.pid`, and waits for `/api/status` readiness.
Stops TORQUE by preferring `POST /api/shutdown`, then falling back to SIGTERM on the recorded PID with exit polling before PID cleanup.
Implements the standalone Node CLI wrapper that resolves versioned help text, normalizes argv and workflow flags into command payloads, and delegates execution plus formatting.
